module_name: Extend core capabilities of Microsoft Sustainability Manager

skills.definition: The ability to configure and utilize Microsoft's dedicated sustainability management solution for tracking and reporting environmental data.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Sustainability Manager is built on Microsoft Power Platform as a model-driven application.

skills.definition: The ability to build, customize, and extend applications using Microsoft's low-code Power Platform framework, specifically model-driven apps.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Sustainability Manager is built on top of Microsoft Power Platform as a model-driven application. You get a set of low-code tools that can help your teams stay agile and build solutions...

skills.definition: The ability to create and customize business intelligence dashboards and visualizations, including integrating them with other Power Platform solutions.
skills.evidence_quote: ...customize your dashboards by using Microsoft Power BI extensions to suit your organizational operations.

extraction.notes: The module focuses on the intersection of Sustainability Manager, Power Platform (specifically model-driven apps), and Power BI integration.

module_name: Optimize your business process with process advisor

skills.definition: The ability to observe, record, and map user actions to identify bottlenecks and document existing business processes for optimization.
skills.evidence_quote: process mining capability in Power Automate helps make that task easier by providing a service that observes your actions and then documents the process for you.

skills.definition: The ability to identify process inefficiencies and recommend or implement automated workflows to improve efficacy.
skills.evidence_quote: process mining identifies bottlenecks and recommends automation solutions with a specific technology.

skills.definition: Proficiency in using Microsoft Power Automate to build, manage, and optimize business workflows and processes.
skills.evidence_quote: knowing the capabilities of specific technology, such as Microsoft Power Automate

extraction.notes: Focused on the intersection of Process Mining (Process Advisor) and Automation within Power Automate.

module_name: Connect syslog data sources to Microsoft Sentinel

skills.definition: The ability to configure data collection agents and rules to ingest, parse, and forward log data from various sources into a centralized monitoring or security workspace.
skills.evidence_quote: You send Syslog log data to the Microsoft Sentinel workspace using an Azure Monitor Agent Data Collection Rule (DCR)... create an Azure Monitor Syslog Data Collection Rule.

skills.definition: The practice of monitoring, detecting, and responding to security threats using centralized security information and event management (SIEM) platforms.
skills.evidence_quote: You're a Security Operations Analyst working at a company that implemented Microsoft Sentinel.

skills.definition: The ability to manage and extend Azure services and management capabilities to on-premises and multi-cloud environments.
skills.evidence_quote: install the Azure Connected Machine agent that enables you to manage your (Windows and) Linux machines hosted outside of Azure on your corporate network with Azure Arc.

extraction.notes: Focused on the operational aspect of getting logs into Sentinel via Arc/AMA, rather than deep parsing logic.

module_name: Apply Networking concepts to Windows containers running on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and AKS Hybrid

skills.definition: The ability to configure, manage, and orchestrate containerized workloads using Kubernetes platforms, including handling networking and scaling complexities.
skills.evidence_quote: When you need to scale a complex system that has multiple containers working together, container management is more challenging. Your solution must incorporate configuration and maintenance for load balancing, orchestration of the deployment process, and support for network connectivity.

skills.definition: The ability to define and implement network configurations for containerized applications, including managing connectivity and load balancing within container orchestration environments.
skills.evidence_quote: Define networking configurations for Kubernetes environments and their pros and cons, then choose the best option for your scenario.

skills.definition: The ability to design and implement applications as a collection of loosely coupled services, leveraging containers for efficient resource usage and scalability.
skills.evidence_quote: Containers are a common development approach for microservice architectures and applications. A containers-based implementation uses hardware efficiently because the security features allow multiple instances to run simultaneously on the same host without affecting each other.

extraction.notes: Focused on the intersection of Kubernetes orchestration and networking for Windows containers on AKS.

module_name: Configure and deploy Teams Phone

skills.definition: Configure and manage Microsoft Teams telephony features, including calling policies, dial plans, and PSTN connectivity options like Calling Plans and Operator Connect.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Calling Plans, Operator Connect, Direct Routing or Teams Phone Mobile PSTN connectivity must be configured before phone numbers can be assigned to users.

skills.definition: Manage enterprise voice services, including PSTN integration, emergency calling compliance, and call routing policies.
skills.evidence_quote: Emergency calling locations are required to meet legal requirements in many countries/regions and must also be configured before users can be assigned phone numbers.

skills.definition: Manage telephony subscriptions and licensing models, such as Microsoft Calling Plans or Operator Connect, to enable voice services.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Calling plans or Operator Connect must be configured before phone numbers can be assigned to users.

extraction.notes: Folded specific PSTN connectivity tasks (Direct Routing, Emergency Calling) into broader Unified Communications and Teams Administration skills as per granularity rules.

module_name: Store local data with SQLite in a .NET MAUI app

skills.definition: The ability to implement and manage local, file-based relational databases within applications for offline data storage and performance optimization.
skills.evidence_quote: To store relational data, you can use a SQLite database... .NET Multi-platform App UI (MAUI) allows you to cache data locally on the device

skills.definition: The ability to build cross-platform applications using the .NET Multi-platform App UI framework.
skills.evidence_quote: .NET Multi-platform App UI (MAUI) allows you to cache data locally on the device on which the application is running.

skills.definition: The ability to design and develop applications for mobile devices, including handling local data storage and performance considerations.
skills.evidence_quote: When you're building mobile applications, it's common to store data locally on a device for performance reasons.

extraction.notes: Focused on the intersection of .NET MAUI development and local data persistence using SQLite.

module_name: Train and evaluate regression models

skills.definition: The ability to build and interpret statistical models that predict continuous numeric outcomes based on input data.
skills.evidence_quote: Regression is where models predict a number. In machine learning, the goal of regression is to create a model that can predict a numeric, quantifiable value, such as a price, amount, size, or other scalar number.

microsoft_surface_forms: predict the expected number of rentals
skills.definition: The practice of using historical data to create models that forecast future numerical events or trends.
skills.evidence_quote: For example, if a company that rents bicycles wants to predict the expected number of rentals on a given day in the future, a regression model can predict this number.

extraction.notes: The module focuses exclusively on the conceptual foundation of regression for predicting numeric values, so skills are limited to Regression Analysis and Predictive Modeling.

module_name: Enable patients to access the Patient Access portal

skills.definition: Configure and manage patient-facing portals to provide access to health data, appointment scheduling, and direct engagement channels.
skills.evidence_quote: Provide patients with access to their health data, appointment scheduling, and information through the Patient Access portal and the Patient Service Center.

skills.definition: Manage and expose patient clinical data and health information through secure digital channels.
skills.evidence_quote: Patient Access provides a portal that helps you give patients the ability to... view their clinical data.

skills.definition: Implement automated chat and messaging solutions to facilitate direct communication between patients and caregivers.
skills.evidence_quote: Patients can engage through automated chat conversations that hands off to your patient service center.

extraction.notes: Focused on the Patient Access service within Microsoft for Healthcare, emphasizing portal configuration, data access, and engagement tools.

module_name: Recurring contract billing in Dynamics 365 Finance

skills.definition: The ability to configure and manage automated billing schedules, contract terms, and revenue forecasting to ensure accurate and timely invoicing.
skills.evidence_quote: Dynamics 365 Finance enables you to customize and implement recurring contract billing features to your organization... automate renewals and allowing flexible pricing models.

skills.definition: The capability to set up, manage, and monitor recurring billing cycles and contract renewals for customers.
skills.evidence_quote: You can use this feature to set up automated contracts, which will supply you with recurring revenue reports.

skills.definition: The skill of using billing data and contract structures to predict future revenue streams and financial performance.
skills.evidence_quote: As a result, you’ll be able to forecast more accurately.

extraction.notes: Focused on the business process of recurring billing within D365 Finance, mapping to Revenue Operations and Subscription Management.

module_name: Explore device encryption features

skills.definition: The ability to use full-disk encryption tools to protect data on storage media against unauthorized physical access.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the benefits of drive encryption with BitLocker.

skills.definition: The ability to configure and leverage hardware-based security modules for secure key storage and system integrity verification.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the benefits of a Trusted Platform Module (TPM).

skills.definition: The ability to implement file-level or volume-level encryption to protect sensitive data from unauthorized access.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the benefits of Encrypted File System.

extraction.notes: Focused on core Windows security features for data protection; mapped BitLocker and TPM to specific security skills rather than generic 'Endpoint Security'.

module_name: Evaluate costs and benefits of AI solutions

skills.definition: The ability to define and calculate measurable business value and financial returns from technology investments.
skills.evidence_quote: Learners explore how to define ROI criteria that reflect measurable business value, technical feasibility, and long-term sustainability.

skills.definition: The ability to assess all direct and indirect costs associated with acquiring, operating, and maintaining a solution over its lifecycle.
skills.evidence_quote: This unit introduces the foundational concepts of Return on Investment (ROI) and Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) in the context of AI solutions.

skills.definition: The ability to evaluate strategic options for software acquisition, including developing custom solutions, purchasing off-the-shelf products, or extending existing systems.
skills.evidence_quote: deciding whether to build, buy, or extend AI components for business processes.

skills.definition: The ability to design and evaluate high-level system structures to meet business requirements and technical constraints.
skills.evidence_quote: This module is designed to equip solution architects and business decision-makers with a comprehensive framework for assessing, justifying, and selecting AI-powered business solutions.

extraction.notes: Focused on the business/financial evaluation aspect of AI rather than technical implementation skills.

module_name: Measure and mitigate risks for a generative AI app in Azure AI Foundry

skills.definition: The ability to implement governance, safety, and ethical controls within generative AI applications to ensure they operate safely and reliably.
skills.evidence_quote: Learn how to measure and mitigate risks for a generative AI app leveraging responsible AI tools and features within Azure AI Foundry.

skills.definition: The practice of configuring and managing content filtering mechanisms to prevent generative AI models from producing harmful, biased, or inappropriate outputs.
skills.evidence_quote: Create and add a content filter

skills.definition: The process of assessing the quality, accuracy, and safety of generative AI outputs using both human-in-the-loop and automated evaluation frameworks.
skills.evidence_quote: Execute a manual evaluation

microsoft_surface_forms: Upload data and create an index
skills.definition: The technical skill of integrating external proprietary data with large language models to ground responses in specific, up-to-date information sources.
skills.evidence_quote: Upload data and create an index

extraction.notes: Focused on the operationalization of GenAI safety (Responsible AI) rather than model training; includes specific tactical skills like Content Filtering and RAG index creation as they are distinct competencies in the GenAI stack.

module_name: Evaluate Windows client installation methods

skills.definition: The ability to plan, execute, and manage the installation and deployment of Windows operating systems on client devices.
skills.evidence_quote: You'll learn scenarios such as whether you're deploying for a new or existing user, replacing a machine, or upgrading the OS are some factors that can determine the installation method.

microsoft_surface_forms: installing and deploying the Windows client
process of installing the Windows client
skills.definition: The process of distributing and installing software packages or operating systems to target machines in an enterprise or consumer environment.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the options available for installing and deploying the Windows client.

microsoft_surface_forms: methods of activation for the Windows client
skills.definition: Managing the legal and technical activation of software licenses to ensure compliance and functionality.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the methods of activation for the Windows client.

extraction.notes: Focused on the operational aspects of Windows deployment, licensing, and installation methods.

module_name: Explore release strategy recommendations

skills.definition: Design and configure automated deployment workflows, including artifact management, approval gates, and release strategies.
skills.evidence_quote: Learn to define release pipeline components and configure artifact sources. Create comprehensive release approval workflows. Implement and configure release gates for quality assurance.

skills.definition: Implement automated processes to deploy software changes to production or staging environments.
skills.evidence_quote: Learn critical release strategy recommendations for automated deployments.

skills.definition: Implement controls and gates to ensure software quality before deployment.
skills.evidence_quote: Implement and configure release gates for quality assurance.

extraction.notes: Focused on Azure DevOps release management concepts; 'Release Management' is the primary skill, with 'Continuous Deployment' and 'Quality Assurance' as supporting competencies.

module_name: Build enterprise AI agents with Java and Spring

skills.definition: Developers can build enterprise-grade applications using the Spring ecosystem, specifically leveraging Spring AI abstractions for AI integration.
skills.evidence_quote: Spring AI is a powerful framework that simplifies the integration of AI capabilities into Java applications.

skills.definition: Developers can build and deploy applications using the Java programming language.
skills.evidence_quote: Build and deploy a Java application and AI agents using Spring AI, Azure OpenAI, and Azure Container Apps with PostgreSQL vector storage.

skills.definition: Professionals can integrate and manage large language models and AI capabilities using Microsoft's Azure OpenAI service.
skills.evidence_quote: In this module, you learn how to build an AI-powered application and AI agents, using Spring AI with Azure OpenAI

skills.definition: Professionals can deploy and manage containerized applications using the serverless container service in Microsoft Azure.
skills.evidence_quote: deploy to Azure Container Apps.

skills.definition: Developers can implement RAG patterns to enhance AI model outputs by integrating external data sources.
skills.evidence_quote: Advisors API: encapsulates generative AI patterns - for example, to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

skills.definition: Professionals can store and query vector embeddings for semantic search using database extensions like pgvector.
skills.evidence_quote: Vector storage integration: built-in support for vector databases, such as `pgvector`.

extraction.notes: Included both the framework (Spring/Java) and the specific Azure services (OpenAI, Container Apps) as they represent distinct technical competencies required for this module.

module_name: Manage device authentication

skills.definition: The practice of verifying the identity of users and devices to control access to organizational resources.
skills.evidence_quote: Besides managing user authentication and protecting user identities, it's also important to cover device authentication when deploying Microsoft Entra ID in your organization.

microsoft_surface_forms: Manage devices joined to Microsoft Entra ID
skills.definition: The process of registering, joining, and maintaining the lifecycle of devices within an identity provider to ensure they are compliant and manageable.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Entra ID allows you to join Windows based devices, as well as to manage them by using somewhat different methods than in Active Directory Domain Services.

skills.definition: Securing endpoints (devices) by integrating them into the central identity system to enforce access policies and protect resources.
skills.evidence_quote: You can also use a device to protect your resources at any time and location.

extraction.notes: Focused heavily on the intersection of Identity (Entra ID) and Device Management (Entra Join). 'Device Management' is treated as a distinct skill from general 'Identity and Access Management' because it involves specific lifecycle actions (joining, managing) distinct from user authentication.

module_name: Create an Azure Content Understanding client application

skills.definition: The ability to build applications that analyze and extract information from multiple content formats, including documents, images, audio, and video, using specialized AI services.
skills.evidence_quote: Azure Content Understanding is a multimodal service that simplifies the creation of AI-powered analyzers that can extract information from multiple content formats, including documents, images, audio files, and videos.

skills.definition: The ability to integrate with external services using standard HTTP protocols and JSON data formats to submit requests and process responses.
skills.evidence_quote: You can develop client applications that use Azure Content Understanding analyzers by using the Python SDK or the REST API; which is the focus of this module.

skills.definition: The ability to write software applications using the Python programming language, including the use of specific software development kits (SDKs) for cloud services.
skills.evidence_quote: In this module, you'll learn how to write code that uses the Python SDK and REST API to submit a

extraction.notes: Focused on the intersection of AI (multimodal analysis) and development (Python/REST integration). Microsoft-specificity is high for the AI service but low for the integration patterns.

module_name: Guided project: Monitor Azure with Service Health and Activity Log alerts

skills.definition: The ability to configure and manage Azure's native monitoring services to track platform health and management operations.
skills.evidence_quote: This guided project focuses on monitoring Azure using Service Health alerts, Activity Log alerts, and action groups.

skills.definition: The ability to detect and respond to Azure platform-level outages and planned maintenance events.
skills.evidence_quote: Service Health alerts watch for platform-level incidents and planned maintenance.

skills.definition: The ability to track and alert on critical management operations, such as resource deletion or configuration changes.
skills.evidence_quote: Activity Log alerts fire when management operations—like resource deletion—occur.

skills.definition: The ability to configure automated notification channels (Action Groups) to alert stakeholders via email or other methods.
skills.evidence_quote: Action groups define who gets notified and how... You set up an action group with email notification

extraction.notes: Focused on the specific Azure Monitor components (Service Health, Activity Log, Action Groups) rather than generic 'monitoring'.

module_name: Describe monitoring tools in Azure

skills.definition: The ability to configure and utilize Azure's native monitoring stack, including Log Analytics workspaces, alerts, and application performance monitoring, to gain visibility into Azure resources and applications.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe Azure Monitor, including Azure Log Analytics, Azure Monitor Alerts, and Application Insights.

skills.definition: The ability to use Azure Advisor to receive personalized recommendations for optimizing Azure resources for reliability, security, performance, and operational excellence.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe the purpose of Azure Advisor.

skills.definition: The ability to monitor Azure service status, receive notifications about outages, and view planned maintenance affecting specific Azure regions or services.
skills.evidence_quote: Describe Azure Service Health.

extraction.notes: All three learning objectives map directly to distinct Microsoft monitoring tools; grouped under observability as they serve the same functional purpose of environment visibility.

module_name: Understand Microsoft Purview eDiscovery

skills.definition: The ability to manage the end-to-end lifecycle of legal investigations, including identifying, preserving, reviewing, and producing electronically stored information (ESI) in a defensible manner.
skills.evidence_quote: Microsoft Purview provides eDiscovery tools that help legal, compliance, and security teams manage this process end to end.

skills.definition: The competency to ensure organizational responses to legal and regulatory requests are consistent, defensible, and complete, mitigating organizational risk.
skills.evidence_quote: Organizations need a consistent, defensible process for identifying, preserving, reviewing, and producing electronically stored information (ESI).

skills.definition: The ability to execute targeted searches across Microsoft 365 services to locate relevant data for investigations or compliance audits.
skills.evidence_quote: With support for role-based access control, content search, legal holds, review sets, and advanced analytics, organizations can respond to investigations with confidence and consistency.

skills.definition: The skill of placing and managing legal holds on data sources to prevent the modification or deletion of electronically stored information during an investigation.
skills.evidence_quote: Review how organizations can manage investigations by preserving content... legal holds

skills.definition: The ability to configure and manage access permissions based on user roles to control who can view, search, or export sensitive investigation data.
skills.evidence_quote: ...controlling access through role-based permissions... support for role-based access control

extraction.notes: Focused on the administrative and procedural aspects of eDiscovery within the Purview suite, emphasizing the workflow from preservation to production.
